title: Breitmann in Battle
content:
آ آ آ آ DER FADER UND DER SON.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I dinks Iï؟½ll go a vightinï؟½ï؟½
آ آ آ آ outshpoke der Breitemann.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Itï؟½s eighdeen hoonderd fordy-eight
آ آ آ آ since I kits swordt in hand;
آ آ آ آ Dese fourdeen years mit Hecker
آ آ آ آ all roostinï؟½ I haf been,
آ آ آ آ Boot now I kicks der Teufel oop
آ آ آ آ and goes for sailinï؟½ in.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½If you go land out-ridinï؟½,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ said Caspar Pickletongue,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Foost ding you knows you cooms across
آ آ آ آ some repels prave and young.
آ آ آ آ Away down Soutï؟½ in Tixey,
آ آ آ آ deyï؟½ll split you like a clamï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½For dat,ï؟½ spoke out der Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½I doos not gare one tam!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Who der Teufel peï؟½s de repels,
آ آ آ آ und vhere dey kits deir sass?
آ آ آ آ If dey make a run on Breitmann
آ آ آ آ heï؟½ll soon let out de gas;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll shplit dem like kartoffels;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll schlog em on de kop;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll set de plackguarts rooninï؟½ so,
آ آ آ آ dey donï؟½t know vhere to shtop.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Und de outshpoke der Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ mit his schlaeger py his side:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Forvarts, my pully landsmen!
آ آ آ آ itï؟½s dime to run and ride;
آ آ آ آ Vill riden, vill vighten
آ آ آ آ der Copitain Iï؟½ll pe,
آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s sporn und horn und saddle now
آ آ آ آ all in de Cavallrie!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Und ash dey rode droo Vinchesder,
آ آ آ آ so herrlich to be seen,
آ آ آ آ Dere coomed some repel cavallrie
آ آ آ آ a ridenï؟½ on de creen;
آ آ آ آ Mit a sassy repel Dootchman
آ آ آ آ an colonel in gommand,
آ آ آ آ Says he, ï؟½Vot Teufel makes you here
آ آ آ آ in dis mein Faderland?

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Youï؟½re dressed oop like a shentleman
آ آ آ آ mit your plackguart Yankee crew,
آ آ آ آ You mudsills and meganics!
آ آ آ آ Der Teufel put you droo!
آ آ آ آ Old Yank, you ought to shtay at home
آ آ آ آ und dake your liddle horn,
آ آ آ آ Mit some oldt voomans for a noorseï؟½
آ آ آ آ der Breitmann laugh mit shkorn.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Und should I trink mein lager beer
آ آ آ آ und roost mine self to home?
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½fe got too many dings like you
آ آ آ آ to mash beneatï؟½ my thoom:
آ آ آ آ In many a fray und fierce foray
آ آ آ آ dis Dootchman will be feared
آ آ آ آ Pefore he stops dis vightinï؟½ trade
آ آ آ آ ï؟½twas dere he grayed his peard.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I pools dat peard out py de roots
آ آ آ آ I gifes him such a dwist
آ آ آ آ Dill all de plood roons out,
آ آ آ آ you tamned old Apolitionist!
آ آ آ آ You creenpacks mit your swordt und vatch,
آ آ آ آ right ofer you moost shell,
آ آ آ آ Und den you goes to Libby stright
آ آ آ آ und after dat to h-ll!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Mein creenpacks and mein schlaeger,
آ آ آ آ I kits ï؟½em in New York,
آ آ آ آ To gife dem up to creenhorns,
آ آ آ آ young man, is not de talk;ï؟½
آ آ آ آ De heroes shtopped deir sassinï؟½ here
آ آ آ آ und grossed deir sabres dwice,
آ آ آ آ Und de vay dese Deutschers vent to vork
آ آ آ آ vos von pig ding on ice.

آ آ آ آ Der younger fetch de older
آ آ آ آ such a gottallmachty shmack
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann dinks he really hears
آ آ آ آ his skool go shplit and crack;
آ آ آ آ Der repel shoomps dwelfe paces back,
آ آ آ آ und so he safe his life:
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann says: ï؟½I guess dem shoomps,
آ آ آ آ you learns dem of your vife.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½If I should learn of vomans
آ آ آ آ I dinks it vere a shame,
آ آ آ آ Bei Gott I am a shentleman,
آ آ آ آ aristograt, and game.
آ آ آ آ My fader vos anoder
آ آ آ آ I lose him fery young
آ آ آ آ Der Teufel take your soul! Coom on!
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll split your vagginï؟½ tongue!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ A Yankee drick der Breitmann dried
آ آ آ آ dat oldt gray-pearded man
آ آ آ آ For ash the repel raised his swordt,
آ آ آ آ beneatï؟½ dat sword he ran.
آ آ آ آ All round der shlim yoong repels vaist
آ آ آ آ his arms oldt Breitmann pound,
آ آ آ آ Und shlinged him down oopon his pack
آ آ آ آ and laidt him on der ground.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Who rubs against olt kittle-pots
آ آ آ آ may keep vhite if he can,
آ آ آ آ Say vot you dinks of vightinï؟½ now
آ آ آ آ mit dis oldt shentleman?
آ آ آ آ Your dime is oop; you got to die,
آ آ آ آ und I your breest vill pe;
آ آ آ آ Pelievï؟½st dou in Morï؟½l Ideas?
آ آ آ آ If so, I lets you free.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I donï؟½t know nix apout ideas
آ آ آ آ no more dan ï؟½pout Saint Paul,
آ آ آ آ Since Iï؟½fe peen down in Tixey
آ آ آ آ I kits no books at all;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m greener ash de clofer-grass;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m shtupid as a shpoon;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m ignoranter ash de nigs
آ آ آ آ for dey takes de Tribune.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Mein faderï؟½s name vas Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ I heard mein mutter say,
آ آ آ آ She read de bapers dat he died
آ آ آ آ after she rooned afay;
آ آ آ آ Dey say he leaf some broperty
آ آ آ آ berhaps ï؟½tvas all a sell
آ آ آ آ If I could lay mein hands on it
آ آ آ آ I likes it mighty vell.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Und vas dy fader Breitmann?
آ آ آ آ Bist du his kit and kin?
آ آ آ آ Denn know dat ich der Breitmann
آ آ آ آ dein lieber Vater bin?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann poolled his hand-shoe off
آ آ آ آ und shooked him py de hand;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Veï؟½ll hafe some trinks on strengtï؟½ of dis
آ آ آ آ or else may I be tamï؟½d!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Oh! fader, how I shlog your kop,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ der younger Breitmann said;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Iï؟½d den dimes sooner had it coom
آ آ آ آ right down on mein own headt!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Oh, never mind dat soon dry oop
آ آ آ آ I shticks him mit a blaster;
آ آ آ آ If I had shplit you like a fish,
آ آ آ آ dat vere an vorse tisasder.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Dis fight did last all afternoon
آ آ آ آ wohl to de fesper tide,
آ آ آ آ Und droo de streets of Vinchesder,
آ آ آ آ der Breitmann he did ride.
آ آ آ آ Vot vears der Breitmann on his hat?
آ آ آ آ De ploom of fictory!
آ آ آ آ Whoï؟½s dat a ridinï؟½ py his side?
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Dis hereï؟½s mein son,ï؟½ says he.

آ آ آ آ How stately rode der Breitmann oop!
آ آ آ آ how lordly he kit down!
آ آ آ آ How glorious from de great pokal
آ آ آ آ he drink de beer so prown!
آ آ آ آ But der Younger bick der parrel oop
آ آ آ آ und schwig him all at one.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Bei Gott! dat settles all his dings
آ آ آ آ I know dou art mein son!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Der one has got a fader;
آ آ آ آ de oder found a child.
آ آ آ آ Bofe ride oopon one war-path now
آ آ آ آ in pattle fierce und vild.
آ آ آ آ It makes so glad our hearts to hear
آ آ آ آ dat dey did so succeed
آ آ آ آ Und damit hat sein Ende
آ آ آ آ DES JUNGEN BREITMANNï؟½S LIED.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Charles G. Leland delivers a powerful performance in "Breitmann in Battle"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
